title: Assignment
description: Learn shorthand ways to update variables.

slides.title: Assignment Operators =
slides.content:
Assignment operators are used to assign values to variables.

You already know the basic assignment operator `=`, which assigns a value to a variable.
`int x = 10;`

slides.title: Addition Assignment += ➕=
slides.content:
If you want to add a value to an existing variable, you can use the `+=` operator.

`int x = 10;`
`x += 5;`

This is exactly the same as writing `x = x + 5;`. It's just a helpful shortcut!

quizData.question: If int score = 20; and then we do score += 10;, what is the new score?
quizData.explanation: score += 10 adds 10 to the existing score of 20, making it 30.

slides.title: Other Shortcuts ✂️
slides.content:
This shortcut works for all arithmetic operators!

* `-=` (e.g. `x -= 3`) represents `x = x - 3`
* `*=` (e.g. `x *= 3`) represents `x = x * 3`
* `/=` (e.g. `x /= 3`) represents `x = x / 3`
* `%=` (e.g. `x %= 3`) represents `x = x % 3`

quizData.question: What is the equivalent long form of health -= 15;?
quizData.explanation: The -= operator subtracts the right operand from the left operand and assigns the result to the left operand.
